location.office_time_required: flexible; in-person events periodically (Flex First policy)

level_signals: 5+ years of post-graduation experience in a technology field
Expected to drive executive-level narratives and build alignment with senior Engineering and Product leaders
Base pay range $178K-$226K depending on state, consistent with Senior IC TPM at a major tech company
Owns large, complex, high-visibility programs end-to-end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of work experience post-graduation in a technology field, preferably as a technical program or project manager, product manager, software engineer (technical domain), or related technical discipline.

Demonstrated ability to create functional specs, set program structure and influence program strategy. Previous experience in managing complex multi-org programs end-to-end, from definition to launch, throughout a software development lifecycle.
A strong communicator with the ability to distill multiple streams of information, convey priorities and risks, and build a narrative for stakeholders.
A strong sense of ownership and intellectual curiosity to understand executional and technical problems in depth, identify potential issues and offer actions to prevent them.
Excellent collaborator and motivator, able to bring a team together to align and deliver on a common goal. Can build trust and partnerships with senior Engineering and Product leaders.
Growth mindset - seeks and is receptive to actionable feedback and growth opportunities.
Takes accountability seriously; can operate independently to deliver responsibilities and milestones on time and within budget.
Self-motivated and thrives in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor's degree in a technology / engineering discipline.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Master's degree in a technology/engineering discipline.

Demonstrated ability to showcase deep technical domain knowledge on the programs that you take on, enabling you to be seen as a strong technical voice in the program and adding significant value towards architecting a program.
Demonstrated ability to drive architectural and strategic decisions at the org leadership level.
Extensive previous experience around creating innovative program management processes/tools/approaches to deliver company outcomes reliably and efficiently.
Experience working with e-commerce solutions at scale.

scope.surface: Enterprise platform for retailer onboarding and growth across Instacart's multi-sided marketplace
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time, high-quality delivery of enterprise retailer launches
Program execution efficiency and quality metrics
core_responsibilities: Lead planning and technical execution of large, complex, high-visibility engineering programs
Drive definition of project requirements and execution plans end-to-end
Own stakeholder communication including priorities, risks, and changes
Foster collaborative team environment and drive process efficiency
Uphold and improve the operational excellence framework for retailer launches

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation ranges explicitly stated across US geographies ($178K-$226K base)
Equity mentioned (new hire + annual refresh grants)
Fully remote-eligible (Flex First policy) with no mandatory office percentage stated
Clear program scope — Enterprise retailer platform — not vague
Cross-functional partners and responsibilities well defined
Role is within an established TPM organization, signaling structural support
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization eligibility not stated anywhere in the JD
Bachelor's degree in technology/engineering listed as hard requirement with no explicit equivalent experience clause
Location pay differential is significant — lowest band ($178K-$188K) vs highest ($214K-$226K) is a ~$38K gap depending on state
Ambiguity around 'Flex First' in-person expectations — described loosely as 'regular in-person events' without specifics

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from compensation range, scope, and years requirement — not explicitly titled 'Senior IC')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated directly; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and multi-sided marketplace language)
location.office_time_required (inferred from Flex First policy description; no hard percentage stated)
compensation.base_range_usd reported as min/max across all US geographies — actual individual offer depends on location
ambiguities: Work authorization and visa sponsorship are entirely unaddressed in the JD — left as 'unclear'

Flex First policy mentions 'regular in-person events' but gives no frequency or office time percentage — unclear if this qualifies as hybrid or remote-us; treated as remote-us given 'Flex First remote work policy' language
Bachelor's degree is listed as a Minimum Qualification but no equivalent experience clause is included — severity set to hard, but some tech companies treat degree requirements loosely in practice
